title: Gilbertiodendron quadrifolium
intro: Gilbertiodendron quadrifolium, also known as African four-leaf, is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of West Africa. The health benefits of G. quadrifolium include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of G. quadrifolium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, G. quadrifolium is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in the form of dried leaves or powder, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
taxonomy: Gilbertiodendron quadrifolium is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its fruit and leaf structure, which are similar to those of other legume species. As a member of the genus Gilbertiodendron, G. quadrifolium is closely related to other species within this group, which are primarily native to tropical regions of Africa. The Fabaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and G. quadrifolium is classified within the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. The precise classification of G. quadrifolium can be further refined at the species level, but its general classification is well-established within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Gilbertiodendron quadrifolium, also known as the African white mahogany, has various medicinal uses. The plant's bark and leaves are traditionally used to treat different health conditions.
